Incomplete foundational coursework may delay your ability to graduate on time.</p><p> </p><p>Visit Department agreements as needed</p><p><br></p><p>Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (<a href=\"https://ua.ucdavis.edu/portal/find-my-advisor\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Find my advisor</a>)</p><p><br></p><p>Note:</p><p><strong>Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:</strong></p><p>AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">General Catalog</a>. Quick reference charts for <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/APCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">AP</a> and <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/IBCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">IB</a>.</p>","groupId":"27ab7645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":5,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p><a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Human development</a> explores the developmental process in humans throughout the life cycle. Biological, psychological, social, and contextual processes on development are studied. Students can choose emphases in Health and Well being across the Lifespan, or Children and Families across the Lifespan.</p><p><br></p><p>Human development majors complete a group of preparatory courses in anthropology, biology, philosophy, psychology, and statistics. Upper division students can design their programs to specialize in one of two track areas. For instance, students can study either Health and Well being, or Children and Families, both of these tracks emphasize a lifespan developmental approach. Students can also pursue the general Human Development track which broadly exposes students to multiple influences on individual development and family processes.</p>","groupId":"5dab7645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>At least one practicum course is required. A second practicum or supervised internship can be used to fulfill the restricted elective requirement for the major. In addition, students can intern in schools, early childhood education or senior centers, hospitals, rehabilitation centers, probation offices, group foster homes, mental health clinics, or as tutors for handicapped or bilingual students. Human development graduates fill a wide variety of positions in preschools, elementary and special educational settings, programs designed for parents, families, and the elderly, as well as governmental jobs related to social services for people of all ages. Students who choose the Health & Well being track can apply to medical school or pursue training for positions in the health sciences. Human development prepares students to pursue advanced degrees in behavioral and social sciences, education, social work, family law, or health sciences.</p>","groupId":"62ab7645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"}]
